options: I don’t find road trips so tough.
Road trips astonish me all the time.
I’m not exactly into road trips.

III. 綜合測驗 說明:以下兩篇短文,共有 10 個空格,為第 22 至 31 題,每題有四個選項,請依各篇短文文意, 選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 ▲ 下篇短文共有 5 個空格,為第 22-26 題,請依短文文意,選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 For decades, the Netherlands has also been known as Holland. However, this is no longer the case now! According to the news release of the Dutch government, 22 January 1, 2020, the Netherlands 22 the only official name of this country in Western Europe. To our surprise, this decision of the Dutch government is not about changing the name of its country but about attracting more tourists to it! Its spokesman told the press, “For years, people have had this 23: the Netherlands is Holland. But it’s not true! The Netherlands is the name of our country, 24 Holland refers to our two provinces (North Holland & South Holland). Nowadays, most tourists come to the Netherlands only to visit Holland and ignore all the other 25 places in it! Our goal is to change this! But we 26 it if people 26 to think the Netherlands and Holland are the same place!”

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 109-3-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: According to the essay, which of the following is TRUE?
options: Old Hal’s Steakhouse delivers its meals for free every day.
Little Nikki’s family lives far away from New York City.
Old Hal’s Steakhouse has been for over a century.

Little Nikki’s mother doesn’t eat any meal at all. ▲閱讀下文,回答第 34-37 題 Kenji is a Japanese exchange student in Taiwan. One day, Dicky, his Taiwanese classmate, had to stay in the hospital for a few days. Kenji was planning to visit Dicky. Before he went, he figured that he should bring Dicky a gift. Finally, he decided to give him some towels, for in his culture, a towel is considered an appropriate gift on most occasions. So he went to a department store and handpicked several exquisite towels. However, on seeing Kenji’s gift, Dicky instantly pulled a long face. Obviously, he got quite upset! Dicky’s response completely threw Kenji off! He really had no clue about what he’d done wrong! Antonio is from Spain. Last year, he flew to Tokyo to visit his friends. One night, he walked into a local ramen restaurant for dinner. It turned out that he really loved the ramen he’d ordered. To thank the chef, he did what most Europeans would do: he gave him a big tip. However, the chef looked unhappy on seeing Antonio’s tip. He shook his head and threw the tip back to Antonio, leaving him wondering what had just happened! Clearly, Kenji didn’t know that in Chinese culture, a towel is a gift exclusively given at a funeral. Likewise, Antonio wasn’t aware that in Japan, tipping someone is impolite or even insulting. That is, both Kenji and Antonio made the mistake of imposing their cultural values on foreigners. We Chinese have an old saying, “ Those who don’t know are not to blame. ” However, this old saying simply doesn’t apply when one is experiencing a foreign culture. In other words, while you’re dealing with foreigners, it is your responsibility to figure out what they think. Never, ever should you do what you think is right and hope they’ll accept it unconditionally. Above all, if you upset them by doing something forbidden in their culture, you can’t just shrug it off and say, “Sorry, I didn’t know!” In this case, it’s definitely your fault because you failed to get to learn about their culture first!
questions.explanation: 依照本文,下列何者為真? 罪他。 (A) 「老哈子牛排館」每天都有免費送餐的服務。 (C) 得罪一個來自外國文化的人,不能用「不知道」 (B) 小妮子一家人住在離紐約市很遠。 當藉口。 (C) 「老哈子牛排館」已經有一世紀以上的歷史。 (D) 在應對外國人時, 有可能會犯了他們所認為的禁忌 。 Question 38-41 (D) 小妮子的母親完全不吃肉。 當一位孩童到了 12 或 13 歲時,就會被視為是青少 Question 34-37 健次是一名在臺灣求學的日本交換學生。有一天他 年,也會開始經歷所謂的「青少年反叛」時期。在這個 的臺灣同學,阿弟仔,必須住院幾天。健次計畫要去看 時期,青少年也許會拒絕去做被告知要做的事情。另外, 他。在他去之前,他想應該帶個禮物給阿弟仔。最後, 他們也會有許多的情緒問題;任何小事都會讓他們情緒 他決定要送他一些毛巾,因為在日本文化中,一般認為 波動劇烈,甚至變得暴力或粗野。 ( 在應付這樣的青少年, 毛巾在大部份的場合中都算是相當適合的禮物。所以他 我們也許會感嘆: 「這小孩真難搞...但願能有人告訴我該 去了趟百貨公司,親手挑選了幾條精緻毛巾。然而,阿 如何是好!」 ) 但你知道狗狗也有 「青少年反叛」 時期嗎? 弟仔一看到健次的禮物,馬上便拉長了臉。很顯然,他 許多養狗人士都知道,狗狗在幼犬的時期最受人喜 相當不高興!阿弟仔的反應讓健次丈二金剛摸不著頭 愛。幼犬不但看起來非常可愛,也會服從主人所下的所 腦!他渾然不知是哪裡做錯! 有指令。在這個時期,主人能毫無問題地訓練狗狗做任 安東尼奧是西班牙人。去年他飛往東京拜訪友人。 何事,像是坐下,或是撿球。然而,當狗狗到了 8 個月 有天晚上他走進一間當地的拉麵餐館吃晚餐。結果他愛 或一歲大的時候,情況便完全改觀!就和人類的青少年 死了他所點的拉麵。為了謝謝餐廳的主廚,他做了件大 一樣,這個時期的狗狗開始漠視主人的指令,即便遵循 部份歐洲人都會做的事:他給了主廚豐厚的小費。然而, 指令,也顯得十分勉強。倘若主人想要加以處罰,狗狗 那位主廚一看見安東尼奧的小費便顯得很不高興。他搖 便會十分火大地跑開。 ( 一些養狗人士在經歷狗狗的「青 搖頭,把小費丟還給安東尼奧,讓安東尼奧完全搞不懂 少年反叛」時,會因為感到挫折而決定加以棄養! ) 發生何事! 目前越來越多人相信動物也和人類一般,享有各項 很明顯,健次不知道在中國文化裡,毛巾只能在葬 權利。也就是說,年幼的狗狗不應該只因為變得有些反 禮中當成禮物贈送。同樣地,安東尼奧也不明白,在日 叛,就被主人棄養。事實上,青少年時期的狗狗就和人 本給小費是相當不禮貌,甚至是侮辱人的舉動。也就是 類的青少年一樣,需要主人的關愛。更重要的是,這些 說,健次與安東尼奧所犯的錯誤,都是把自己本國的文 狗還需要被耐心對待。因此,在飼養這樣的狗狗時,主 化價值觀,強加在外國人士身上。 人必須多花時間陪伴。此外,主人也應該用獎賞代替處 我們中國人有句老話: 「不知者無罪。」然而,這句 罰。最重要的一點是,主人要記得一件事:狗狗的「青 老話並不適用於體驗他國文化。換言之,在與他國人士 少年反叛時期」終會過去。所以主人要做的,便是等待 打交道時,就有責任搞清楚對方的思維。絕不能做自認 這一天的到來。 為是對的事情,然後希望對方無條件接受。最重要的是,

questions.q: Why does the author think the old Chinese saying, “ Those who don’t know are not to blame. ” doesn’t apply?
options: Experiencing a foreign culture, one is responsible for the foreigners’ mistakes.
We can’t blame someone for not understanding a foreign culture enough.
Not knowing is no excuse for upsetting someone from a foreign culture.

In handling foreigners, it’s possible to do something they think is forbidden. ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 38-41 題 When a child reaches the age of 12 or 13, (s)he may be considered a teenager, and (s)he may begin to go through the stage of the so-called “teenage rebellion.” Thus, (s)he may refuse to do what (s)he’s been told. In addition, (s)he may have lots of emotional trouble; any trifle may cause him or her to get dramatically emotional, or even violent and brutal. (In handling such a teenager, one may tend to say, “This child is really a handful!... I wish someone could tell me what to do!”) But do you know that a dog may also have its “teenage rebellion” period? Plenty of dog owners know that a dog is most adorable when it’s still a puppy. Not only does it look very cute but it also follows every order it’s given. At this stage, its master almost has no problem training it to do anything, including sitting down, or fetching a ball. However, when it becomes 8 months or one year old, things are completely different! Like a human teenager, it starts to ignore its master’s orders, or at least follow them very hesitantly. When its master wants to punish it, it may run away angrily. (In experiencing their dogs’ “teenage rebellion,” some owners may get frustrated and decide to get rid of them!) Nowadays, more and more people believe that animals, like humans, have their own rights. That is, a young dog doesn’t deserve to be abandoned only because it has become a bit more rebellious. In fact, a teenage dog, like a human teenager, needs its master’s love and care. Above all, it needs to be treated with patience. Therefore, when keeping such a dog, the owner must spend more time with it. In addition, (s)he should try to reward it rather than punish it. Most of all, (s)he must remember one thing: a dog’s “teenage rebellion” will pass one day. So all (s)he has to do is wait it out.

A dog’s teenage rebellion is something that will last forever. 二、非選擇題 ( 第 I 到第 III 題,每題 6 分,共 18 分 ) I. 填充 說明: ▲請依據中文提示,將試題內空格  、  答案之完整單字 ( 含提示之字首 ),分別作答於答 案卷之作答欄  、  之指定範圍內以完成句子。 ▲請勿抄題,每格限填一字,超過一字者視為錯誤,不予計分。
